rationale: The citing paper uses Kivetz and Simonson (2002) in its core theoretical framing, not just as a background list citation. It summarizes that paper's key finding (effort in loyalty programs shifts preference toward luxury rewards via guilt reduction) and uses it to motivate the broader claim that guilt-reducing mechanisms can increase indulgent choice. In the discussion, the authors explicitly position their contribution as showing a similar effect through a different antecedent (prior virtuous intent), indicating a meaningful build-on relation.
relation_description: Khan and Dhar (2006) build on Kivetz and Simonson's guilt-reduction account of luxury preference by proposing and testing a new licensing pathway: prior virtuous choices boost self-concept and increase indulgent choices. The cited paper serves as prior evidence for the indulgence-justification mechanism that the citing paper extends beyond loyalty-program effort contexts.
